news.title: 🇪🇸 pedro sánchez is positioning himself as a leader of a global socialist revival, vowing to “twist
news.summary:
🇪🇸 pedro sánchez is positioning himself as a leader of a global socialist revival, vowing to “twist the arms” of far-right forces.

according to politico, sánchez used a left-wing summit in barcelona to 랠리 key leftist leaders against the rise of the far right and to strengthen his international profile.

at the summit, he accused donald 트럼프 of under채굴 multilateral institutions through 관세s and the war in the middle east. 

he also claimed that while far-right groups are organizing internationally, their numbers reflect “weakness,” not strength.

sánchez is reportedly seeking to expand his influence in europe and is eyeing the position of president of the european council, currently held by antónio costa.

news.title:
very interesting reading


palantir (@palantirtech)

because we get asked a lot.

the technological
news.summary:
very interesting reading


palantir (@palantirtech)

because we get asked a lot.

the technological republic, in brief.

1. silicon valley owes a moral debt to the country that made its rise possible. the engineering elite of silicon valley has an affirmative obligation to participate in the defense of the nation.

2. we must rebel against the tyranny of the apps. is the iphone our greatest creative if not crowning achievement as a civilization? the object has changed our lives, but it may also now be limiting and constraining our sense of the possible.

3. free email is not enough. the decadence of a culture or civilization, and indeed its ruling class, will be forgiven only if that culture is capable of delivering economic growth and security for the public.

4. the limits of soft power, of 급상승ing rhetoric alone, have been exposed. the ability of free and democratic societies to prevail requires somETHing more than moral appeal. it requires hard power, and hard power in this century will be built on software.

5. the question is not whETHer a.i. weapons will be built; it is who will build them and for what purpose. our adversaries will not pause to indulge in theatrical debates about the merits of developing technologies with critical military and national security applications. they will proceed.

6. national service should be a universal duty. we should, as a society, seriously consider moving away from an all-volunteer force and only fight the next war if everyone shares in the risk and the cost.

7. if a u.s. marine asks for a better rifle, we should build it; and the same goes for software. we should as a country be capable of continuing a debate about the appropriateness of military action abroad while remaining unflinching in our commitment to those we have asked to step into harm’s way.

8. public servants need not be our priests. any business that compensated its employees in the way that the 연준eral government compensates public servants would struggle to survive.

9. we should show far more grace towards those who have subjected themselves to public life. the eradication of any space for forgiveness—a jettisoning of any tolerance for the complexities and contradictions of the human psyche—may leave us with a cast of characters at the helm we will grow to regret.

10. the psychologization of modern politics is leading us astray. those who look to the political arena to nourish their soul and sense of self, who rely too heavily on their internal life finding expression in people they may never meet, will be left disappointed.

11. our society has grown too eager to hasten, and is often gleeful at, the demise of its enemies. the vanquishing of an opponent is a moment to pause, not rejoice.

12. the atomic age is ending. one age of deterrence, the atomic age, is ending, and a new era of deterrence built on a.i. is set to begin.

13. no other country in the history of the world has advanced progressive values more than this one. the united states is far from perfect. but it is easy to forget how much more opportunity exists in this country for those who are not hereditary elites than in any other nation on the planet.

14. american power has made possible an extraordinarily long peace. too many have forgotten or perhaps take for granted that nearly a century of some version of peace has prevailed in the world without a great power military conflict. at least three generations — billions of people and their children and now grandchildren — have never known a world war.

15. the postwar neutering of germany and japan must be undone. the defanging of germany was an overcorrection for which europe is now paying a heavy price. a similar and highly theatrical commitment to japanese pacifism will, if maintained, also threaten to shift the balance of power in asia.

16. we should applaud those who attempt to build where the market has failed to act. the culture almost snickers at musk’s interest in grand narrative, as if billionaires ought to simply stay in their lane of enriching themselves . . . . any curiosity or genuine interest in the value of what he has created is essentially dismissed, or perhaps lurks from beneath a thinly veiled scorn.

17. silicon valley must play a role in addressing violent crime. many politicians across the united states have essentially shrugged when it comes to violent crime, abandoning any serious efforts to address the problem or take on any risk with their constituencies or donors in coming up with SOLutions and experiments in what should be a desperate bid to save lives.

18. the ruthless exposure of the private lives of public figures drives far too much talent away from government service. the public arena—and the shallow and petty assaults against those who dare to do somETHing other than enrich themselves—has become so unforgiving that the republic is left with a significant roster of ineffectual, empty vessels whose ambition one would forgive if there were any genuine belief structure lurking within.

19. the caution in public life that we unwittingly encourage is corrosive. those who say nothing wrong often say nothing much at all.

20. the pervasive intolerance of religious belief in certain circles must be resisted. the elite’s intolerance of religious belief is perhaps one of the most telling signs that its political project constitutes a less open intellectual movement than many within it would claim.

21. some cultures have produced vital advances; others remain dysfunctional and regressive. all cultures are now equal. criticism and value judgments are forbidden. yet this new dogma glosses over the fact that certain cultures and indeed subcultures . . . have produced wonders. others have proven middling, and worse, regressive and harmful.

22. we must resist the shallow temptation of a vacant and hollow pluralism. we, in america and more broadly the west, have for the past half century resisted 디파이ning national cultures in the name of inclusivity. but inclusion into what?

excerpts from the #1 new york times bestseller the technological republic: hard power, soft belief, and the future of the west, by alexander c. karp & nicholas w. zamiska

techrepublicbook.com



— https://nitter.net/palantirtech/status/2045574398573453312#m

news.title:
layerzero: kelpdao loses ~$290m in exploit, attributed to dprk’s lazarus group

layerzero reported t
news.summary:
layerzero: kelpdao loses ~$290m in exploit, attributed to dprk’s lazarus group

layerzero reported that on april 18, 2026, kelpdao suffered an exploit resulting in losses of approximately $290m, preliminarily attributed to dprk’s lazarus group (tradertraitor). the attack poisoned downstream rpcs used by the dvn and, combined with ddos, forced failover, causing the dvn to validate transactions that never occurred, without exploiting protocol or key vulnerabilities. the incident stemmed from kelpdao’s 1/1 dvn setup, creating a single point of failure; impact was limited to rsETH with no broader contagion. affected rpcs have been replaced and the layerzero labs dvn is now operational.
nitter.net/layerzero_core/status/…

news.summary:

sulaiman ahmed (@shaykhsulaiman)

ceasefire update: iran-u.s draft proposal

✅ iran agrees to a 15-year enrichment suspension, with the exception of research reactors for medical isotopes.

✅ iran’s stockpile of 60% and 20% uranium would be partially converted to reactor fuel, and partially ‘downblended’ to a lower enrichment level. however, it won’t leave iran.

✅ full iaea supervison on iran’s nuclear sites.

✅ opening the strait of hormuz, with the possibility of collecting tolls.

✅ the u.s. will offer iran phased 제재 relief

✅ the u.s. will unfreeze all iranian assets currently frozen abroad, amounting to $20 billion or more.

✅ the u.s. will commit to a non-aggression pact with iran that is ensured through a un national security council reSOLution and a treaty ratified by congress.

✅ the u.s. will withdraw all its military forces from the persian gulf.

ai is eating the manufacturing narrative. 

semiconductors, networking gear, power and cooling equipment. the stuff that keeps data centers alive. that's what's driving this.  

computer and electronic product output up 7.7% last year. not because of 관세s. because the world needs ai infrastructure and america builds it well.  

reindustrialization is happening. just not the kind anyone's taking credit for.

anti-drone camo because tiny uavs see everything? in progress.

this is the quiet lab making sure tomorrow's SOLdier survives not the last war, but the one america hasn't fought yet.

news.title:
the us wage growth gap is historically wide:

after-tax wages for higher-income households rose +5.6
news.summary:
the us wage growth gap is historically wide:

after-tax wages for higher-income households rose +5.6% yoy in march on a 3-month moving average basis, the highest in at least 3 years.

at the same time, lower-income earners saw their wages grow just+1.0% yoy, near the lowest in at least 3 years.

this marks the widest gap between the highest and lowest earners since data began in 2015.

by comparison, middle-income wages grew +2.0% yoy last month, near the lowest since mid-2024.

meanwhile, us cpi 인플레이션 jumped +3.3% yoy in march, meaning lower and middle-income households are seeing their real wages decline.

in other words, 인플레이션 is eating all of the wage gains for the bottom two-thirds of american households.

asset owners are the only winners.

news.title:
the global smartphone market is facing a historic decline:

global smartphone shipments are set to f
news.summary:
the global smartphone market is facing a historic decline:

global smartphone shipments are set to fall -13% yoy in 2026, or -160 million units, to ~1.1 billion, according to idc.

this marks a sharp drop from a +2% growth in 2025 and +6% in 2024.

the decline is being driven by an unprecedented memory chip shortage that is inflating component costs across the industry.

as a result, smartphone makers are discontinuing unprofitable entry-level models and pushing consumers toward higher-priced devices.

last year, ~170 million smartphones shipped for under $100, a segment currently uneconomical to maintain.

the shortage is expected to persist into mid-2027, and even when supply returns, memory prices are unlikely to fall back to 2025 levels.

the days of cheap smartphones are over.

지금 미국이랑 협상하는 쪽은 이란 외교부 라인인데, 여기는 협상하고 싶어해.

news.summary:
지금 이란 관련 헤드라인들 범람하는데,  결론은 하나야. 

지금 미국이랑 협상하는 쪽은 이란 외교부 라인인데, 여기는 협상하고 싶어해.  

그런데 지금 이란 실세는 군부인데, 여기는 막가파야.   

그래서 무슨 협상 재기니, 임박이니 그런 헤드라인은 외교부랑 이야기 해서 나오는거고, 

계속 봉쇄니 지상전 가능성 올라가느니 어쩌구는 군부쪽이야. 

이거 2개가 동시에 나오고 있어서 머가리 뜨거운 상황이야.  

결론만 딱 말하면, 금요일 보다는 상황이 나빠진게 맞아.  

안다스탠?

트럼프가 방금 트루소셜에 올린 글. 

iran killing machine

살인기계라는 표현까지...

글이 매우 길군요... 

'인내심을 시험중'인듯 합니다.

그리고 문장
news.summary:
트럼프가 방금 트루소셜에 올린 글. 

iran killing machine

살인기계라는 표현까지...

글이 매우 길군요... 

'인내심을 시험중'인듯 합니다.

그리고 문장 중간에 대문자로 윽박 지르기 시작했습니다.

"no more mr. nice guy!" 

"it's time for the iran killing machine to end!"

-

1. "a total violation of our ceasefire agreement"

휴전 위반 공식 선언. 

협상 틀을 자신이 먼저 깨는 명분을 만들어두는 단계. 

"내가 먼저가 아니라 쟤들이 먼저 깼다"는 프레임.

-

2. "many of them were aimed at a french ship, and a freighter from the united kingdom"

미국 배가 아닌 프랑스-영국 배를 공격했다는 걸 강조. 

nato 동맹을 끌어들이기 위한 명분 쌓기. 

"이건 미국만의 문제가 아니라 서방 전체의 문제"라는 프레임으로 넘어가려는 것이죠.

-

3. "$500 million dollars a day! the united states loses nothing"

이란이 하루 5억 달러 손실, 미국은 잃는 거 없다는 주장. 

그리고 "많은 배들이 지금 텍사스, 루이지애나, 알래스카로 향하고 있다"는 말...

셰일 증산, 수출 확대 카드를 이미 깔아놨다는 뜻...

유가 관리를 위한 내부 준비가 끝났다는 신호.

-

4. "knock out every single power plant, and every single bridge, in iran" 

가장 무서운 문장. 

발전소와 다리 전부. 

핵시설 타격 수준을 넘어선, 민간 인프라 전면 타격 선언. 

이란 전체를 석기시대로 돌려보내겠다는 의미를 다시 꺼냈습니다.

-

트럼프 트루소셜 원문 번역:

이란이 어제 호르무즈 해협에서 총격을 가하기로 결정했다. 

휴전 협정에 대한 전면적인 위반이다! 

많은 총알이 프랑스 선박과 영국 화물선을 향해 발사됐다. 

참 좋은 행동이었지, 안 그런가? 

우리 대표단이 파키스탄 이슬라마바드로 갈 예정이다. 

내일 저녁 도착해서 협상에 임할 것이다. 

이란은 최근 해협을 봉쇄한다고 발표했는데, 

이게 이상한 게, 우리 봉쇄가 이미 해협을 막아놓았기 때문이다. 

이란은 자기도 모르게 우릴 돕고 있는 셈이고, 

해협이 닫혀서 손해 보는 건 바로 이란이다. 

하루에 5억 달러씩! 

미국은 아무것도 잃지 않는다. 

오히려 많은 배들이 지금 이 순간에도 미국 텍사스, 루이지애나, 알래스카로 향하고 있다. 

irgc 덕분에 말이다, 늘 "터프 가이"가 되고 싶어 하는 양반들! 

우리는 아주 공정하고 합리적인 협상안을 제시하고 있고, 

이란이 받아들이기를 바란다. 

왜냐... 받아들이지 않으면, 

미국은 이란의 모든 발전소, 모든 다리를 파괴할 것이기 때문이다.

이제 미스터 나이스 가이는 없다!

(no more mr. nice guy!) 

그들은 빠르게, 쉽게 무너질 것이다. 

협상을 받아들이지 않는다면, 

해야 할 일을 하는 것이 나의 영광이 될 것이다. 

이 일은 지난 47년 동안 다른 대통령들이 이란에 했어야 했던 일이다. 

이란 살인 기계(iran killing machine)를 끝낼 시간이다!




돈스 (@dons_korea)

x.com/i/article/204580177767…



— https://nitter.net/dons_korea/status/2045819690073944309#m

news.title: ukraine launched a massive drone swarm attack against the oil terminal in the russia’s black sea por
news.summary:
ukraine launched a massive drone swarm attack against the oil terminal in the russia’s black sea port of tuapse tonight

massive fires have broken out across the terminal after several oil tanks and other oil infrastructure were hit

russia has major problems exporting oil by sea

video

news.title:
major protest against alleged election fraud in peru 🇵🇪 

the supporters of the right-wing candidate
news.summary:
major protest against alleged election fraud in peru 🇵🇪 

the supporters of the right-wing candidate @rlopezaliaga1 “porky” have taken to the streets tonight after a presidential election marred by irregularities.

with 93% of the votes counted, porky is on 3rd place with 11.9% of the votes while the left-wing candidate roberto sanchez is on second place with 12.0%.

the authorities now say that they won’t be able provide a final result before mid-may or nearly 5 weeks after the first round of the election took place.

many polling stations in lima, a porky stronghold, opened late, or not at all, on election day, creating huge lines in front of the polling stations that were opened. porky accuses the electoral commission of being in the hands of peruvian leftists and trying to sabotage his campaign at any cost.

porky or sanchez will face the center-right candidate keiko fujimori in the runoff on june 7th as she won the first round with around 17% of the votes
